step.title: Discovery and Scoping
step.goal: Identify what to extract knowledge from and scope the extraction appropriately. Good scoping is critical - too broad yields shallow results, too narrow misses patterns.

substep.title: Identify Source Material
substep.ask:
What should we extract knowledge from?
**Source types:** - **Code** - Implementation patterns, conventions, architecture - **Documents** - PRDs, specs, guides, READMEs - **Prompts** - Existing agent/skill prompts that work well - **Conversations** - Chat logs with useful decisions/patterns - **Examples** - Reference implementations, templates
Please provide: 1. Path(s) to source material 2. Brief description of what it is

substep.title: Determine Knowledge Type
substep.ask:
What kind of knowledge are we extracting?
**patterns** - Recurring solutions (error handling, API design) **conventions** - Standards and naming rules **templates** - Reusable structures with placeholders **guidelines** - Decision frameworks and best practices **vocabulary** - Domain terms and definitions
Which type best describes what you want to extract?

substep.title: Define Scope
substep.action:
Based on source and type, define extraction scope:
- What specific patterns/knowledge to look for? - What should be excluded? - How deep should analysis go? - Who will consume this knowledge? (agents, humans, both)
substep.ask:
Let's scope this extraction:
1. **Focus areas** - What specifically should we extract? 2. **Exclusions** - What should we ignore? 3. **Depth** - Surface patterns or deep analysis? 4. **Audience** - Who will use this knowledge?

substep.title: Summarize Discovery

section.title: Source Selection

rules.rule: Prefer multiple examples over single source (reveals patterns)
Choose sources that represent "good" - what you want to replicate
Include context (why was this created, what problem did it solve)
Recent sources often better than old (current practices)

section.example: "Extract from our 5 most successful PRDs to find what makes them effective" "Analyze the 3 agent prompts that users like best"
"Extract from all files in the repo" (too broad) "Extract from this one file" (may not reveal patterns)

section.title: Scoping Guidelines

rules.rule: Narrow scope = deeper, more actionable knowledge
Broad scope = surface-level, may miss nuance
Define what "success" looks like for extraction

section.example: Narrow: "Error handling patterns in our API layer" Broad: "All patterns in the codebase" (likely too broad)

section.title: Audience Consideration
section.description: Who will consume this knowledge affects format and depth.

audiences.audience: - Structure for LLM consumption - Include examples for few-shot learning - Clear rules with emphasis keywords
- More explanation and context - Visual organization (headers, lists) - Rationale for decisions
- Balance structure and readability - Include both rules and explanations
